rationale: The citing paper references Deng and Mela (2018) in two framing sections, including as the notable marketing example that models media viewing in continuous time. It then positions its own contribution as adding a broader continuous-time product-usage model with multiple decisions and latent states (habit and satiation), plus policy counterfactuals in gaming. Although it does not directly reuse Deng and Mela's empirical setting or targeting design, the citation meaningfully anchors the methodological positioning and incremental claim.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the continuous-time consumer-usage modeling line exemplified by Deng and Mela (2018) from TV viewing/ad avoidance to a richer product-usage framework in online gaming. It builds on that precedent to model additional usage decisions and managerial interventions for excessive use.
